title: A Search for Mahanaim
author: Robert A. Coughenour
publisher: Bulletin of the American Schools of Oriental Research (BASOR) 273, pp. 57-66
allowedUseNotes: Copyrighted journal article. Project stores its own summary of the candidate-site discussion only.
summary: Surveys candidate identifications for biblical Mahanaim in the Jabbok (Zarqa) valley of Transjordan. Coughenour argues for Tell ed-Dahab el-Gharbi (the western mound of Tulul adh-Dhahab) partly on the hypothesis that Mahanaim served as a regional iron-industry center from the time of Solomon to Jeroboam I, given the site's position relative to nearby iron-bearing deposits, and discusses Tell Hajjaj as the strongest competing alternative site. Used here as an early statement that the identification of Mahanaim remains an open, debated question with more than one live candidate, predating the 2024 Finkelstein/Ornan proposal (see finkelstein-ornan-2024-mahanaim) that later revived interest in the same western Tulul adh-Dhahab mound.
confidenceNotes: Seeded from secondary summaries/citations of this article (Wikipedia 'Tulul adh-Dhahab', drbill.net 'Mahanaim Reconsidered' page, academic citations in the 2024 Finkelstein/Ornan article) rather than direct reading of the original BASOR piece. TO VERIFY: the article's own framing/confidence level and its full list of alternative candidate sites once a copy is consulted directly.
